stories.title: Moscow invites far-right German politicians to ‘Putin’s Davos’
stories.summary: BERLIN — An adviser to Russian President Vladimir Putin has invited politicians from the Alternative for Germany (AfD) party to an economic forum in June in an illustration of how the Kremlin is cultivating ties with Germany’s far right. At least two AfD members of the Bundestag, Germany’s lower house of parliament — lawmakers Markus Frohnmaier and Steffen Kotré — are set to attend the St. Petersburg International Economic Forum after receiving invitations, according to three party politicians w

stories.title: ‘I don’t think about Americans’ financial situation,’ says Trump amid Iran talks
stories.summary: With US inflation at a three-year high, US president insisted he’s not focused on economic hardship sparked by the conflict Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email Donald Trump has said the growing financial pressure inflicted on Americans by the war on Iran is “not even a little bit” motivating him to make a peace deal with Tehran. With US inflation at a three-year high , and fuel costs still climbing after a sharp rise in oil prices, the US president said on Tuesday that he is not fo

stories.title: Can Hungary’s Magyar​ deliver on his promise​s of reform a​nd restore a relationship to the EU?
stories.summary: The country’s new leadership has pledged to reverse years of democratic backsliding, but they must act quickly • Don’t get This Is Europe delivered to your inbox? Sign up here Under blue skies on Saturday, crowds cheered as the EU flag was raised on the facade of the Hungarian parliament after a long absence. It was a powerful symbol on the day Péter Magyar was sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister , with a declaration that Hungarians had given his party a mandate to launch “a new chapter” in the

stories.title: Czech police hunt thief who stole 800-year-old skull of saint from church
stories.summary: Suspect was seen on fuzzy security photo running between benches of church carrying skull, police say Czech police are hunting a thief who snatched the 800-year-old skull of a saint from a display box in a church and ran away with the relic. A fuzzy security camera photo released late on Tuesday appeared to show a figure dressed in black carrying what police said was the skull of Saint Zdislava of Lemberk. Continue reading...

stories.summary: A volt Építési és Közlekedési Minisztérium épületében , Lázár János egykori munkahelyén lesz az irodája.

stories.summary: A blog is megjegyezte, hogy korábban még bizonytalanabbul nyilatkoztak uniós körökben a hitelrészről, de most úgy látják, „nagyon jól haladunk”, a jövő héten újabb delegáció jönne Budapestre.

stories.title: Adatokat gyűjtenek be a Győr-Szolnál a rendőrök a lakásalap ügyében
stories.summary: Nyomozók jelentek meg Győr városi mamutcégénél, miután Pintér Bence polgármester 1,7 milliárd forint eltűnését gyanítva feljelentést tett februárban. A cég vezérigazgatója néhány napja lemondott.

stories.summary: Previously less optimistic EU officials have now said that “we are making very good progress,” with another EU delegation due in Budapest next week.

stories.title: Bódis Kriszta szerint nincs semmiféle titok akörül, hogy miért nem lett szociális miniszter
stories.summary: Az író, dokumentumfilmes a Facebook-oldalán értekezett arról, mi lesz a feladata, és mivel foglalkozik most.

stories.title: Nem szoktunk hozzá, hogy egy normális országban élünk, de próbáljuk meg, mert itt van most rá a lehetőség
stories.summary: Pósfai Mihály, a Magyar Tudományos Akadémia újonnan megválasztott elnöke interjúnkban azt mondta, számára nincs jelentősége annak, hogy rokona a belügyminiszter, családon belül nem szoktak protekciózni. Nem tartja problémának, ha az MTA tagjai politikai állásfoglalást tesznek, és a kiválóságokon kívül szeretné támogatni a kutatói derékhadat is.

stories.summary: A francia hatóságok senkit sem engedtek leszállni a Bordeaux-ban kikötött hajóról, amin eddig ötven betegről tudni.

stories.title: Jó oka volt rá Magyar Péternek, hogy megdöntötte a kormányalakítás rekordját
stories.summary: Mindössze 740 órával az április 12-i választás urnazárása után szimbolikus helyszínen, Ópusztaszeren tartják az első kormányülést. A feszes menetrend ellenére volt egy átmeneti időszak, amikor Magyar Péter miniszterelnökként még a leköszönő kormány tagjainak adott utasítást. Gyors döntésekre és sűrű nyárra készülnek.

stories.title: Egy autókereskedőtől is kedvezményt kért magának a korrupcióval gyanúsított, volt fideszes képviselő
stories.summary: Bóna Zoltánt nemcsak azzal gyanúsítják, hogy papíron a halászteleki polgármester 70 éves anyját foglalkoztatta maga mellett az országgyűlési frakcióban.

stories.title: Orbán Balázs: Tegyünk úgy, mintha egy normális, civilizált országban lennénk
stories.summary: A Patrióta műsorában beszélt az általa vezetett kampány kudarcáról, a Fidesz jövőjéről és a Magyar Péter által bemutatott minisztériumokról.

stories.summary: A külügyminiszter azt mondta, folyamatosan kapcsolatban vannak az ottani főkonzullal, és be fognak számolni a fejleményekről.

stories.title: Először ülésezik Magyar Péter kormánya
stories.summary: Ópusztaszeren tartja első ülését a Tisza-kormány, és a miniszterelnök beharangozója szerint belecsapnak a sűrűjébe. Aszály, Alaptörvény-módosítások, állami cégek azonnali átvilágítása és még számos téma napirendre kerülhet. Élőben mutatjuk a kormányülést követő sajtótájékoztatót, majd Király Andrással és Hanula Zsolttal értékeljük az elhangzottakat.

stories.title: EU approves sanctions on Israeli settlers after Hungarian backing
stories.summary: The sanctions — which include asset freezes and travel bans and will also target Hamas members — come amid growing international criticism of Israel’s settlement expansion and escalating violence in the occupied territories.

stories.title: Trump has a weak hand to play in summit with Xi
stories.summary: President Donald Trump is arriving in Beijing in a role that he isn’t used to — a supplicant asking for favors. The White House has said the meeting between Trump and Chinese leader Xi Jinping on Thursday and Friday will  focus on trade, fentanyl  and the war in Iran — all areas where the Trump administration has had little luck getting deals or concessions from China. That means Trump will be hard-pressed to deliver the big outcomes he has promised for weeks. Trump  pitched the m

stories.title: UK faces multi-billion pound bill for nationalizing British Steel
stories.summary: LONDON — Ministers have already spent hundreds of millions keeping British Steel afloat. Nationalizing the embattled steelmaker is set to cost taxpayers billions more, multiple steel industry figures have told POLITICO. Without a commercial buyer in sight, taking on the loss-making firm could see ministers forced to spend billions transforming British Steel’s Scunthorpe steel mill to a modern electric arc furnace fit for the future. The move to full nationalization, set out in Wednesday’s king’s

stories.title: From Streeting to Burnham, here are Starmer’s rivals to run Britain
stories.summary: LONDON — Keir Starmer is facing the biggest rebellion of his time in office. And his rivals are circling. All eyes were on Health Secretary Wes Streeting Wednesday amid briefings he will resign from the government — and trigger a leadership challenge against the British prime minister. Two Labour MPs, including one close to the health secretary, told POLITICO they had been informed Streeting plans to resign and challenge the PM. “Wes is the Health Secretary, he is proud of his record of falling

stories.title: UK watchdog issues first-ever suicide forum fine
stories.summary: LONDON — The U.K.’s online safety regulator Ofcom on Wednesday fined an online suicide forum provider £950,000 for failing to comply with illegal content duties under the Online Safety Act . The penalty comes 13 months after the regulator launched an investigation, the first under the legislation and against this kind of site.   “Given the ongoing risk of harm, we are using all powers available to us to protect the public,” Director of Enforcement Suzanne Cater said in a statemen

stories.title: 1MDB financier Jho Low seeks pardon from Donald Trump
stories.summary: Fugitive faces charges including corruption and money laundering in US and Malaysia for role he allegedly played in scandal The fugitive Malaysian financier Jho Low, a central figure in the multibillion-dollar scandal at the state fund 1Malaysia Development Berhad (1MDB), is reportedly seeking a pardon from the US president, Donald Trump. Low faces multiple charges including corruption and money laundering in the US and Malaysia for the important role he allegedly played in the misappropriation

stories.summary: Senator accused of crimes against humanity by international criminal court has holed up in building to evade arrest Gunshots have been fired in the Philippine senate, as a senator who is wanted by the international criminal court (ICC) remained holed up in the building to evade arrest. stories.title: Argentina sees vast protests against Milei's university cuts
stories.summary: Organizers estimated that some 600,000 students, university staff, union members and opposition supporters attended the protest in Buenos Aires alone.

stories.summary: Gunshots have been reported inside the Philippine Senate as armed troops entered the building. The chaos comes as one senator faces imminent arrest over an ICC warrant.

stories.summary: The foreign minister says the "distressed" Ghanaians registered with the embassy in Pretoria to be evacuated.

stories.title: Hamas 'systematically' used sexual violence in 7 October attacks, report finds
stories.summary: An Israeli commission's two-year investigation concludes Hamas systematically used sexual violence on 7 October and against hostages it held in Gaza, laying groundwork for prosecution.

stories.title: Trump-Xi summit raises a terrifying prospect for America and Europe: Chinese cars
stories.summary: The walls that once kept Chinese electric vehicles out of the western economies are quickly developing some major cracks. That’s made the U.S. auto industry and lawmakers nervous that President Donald Trump’s trip to Beijing for a Thursday summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping could accelerate the entry of cheap EVs, wiping out the nascent U.S. EV sector at a time when fuel costs are soaring because of the U.S. war against Iran and rising car prices are souring public sentiment. “The only thi

stories.title: The lame duck prime minister’s survival guide
stories.summary: LONDON — Boris Johnson once said that “when the herd moves, it moves.” Keir Starmer is trying to stop it trampling him alive. The British prime minister’s legislative agenda for the year ahead was delivered in parliament this morning by King Charles III — but minutes before he sat down, news leaked of a planned challenge to his leadership from Health Secretary Wes Streeting. stories.title: Bahamas re-elects Progressive Liberal party’s Philip Davis as prime minister

stories.title: Head of Microsoft’s Israel branch to step down after inquiry into dealings with Israeli military
stories.summary: The inquiry came after the Guardian revealed Israel used company technology to support mass surveillance of Palestinian phone calls The head of Microsoft ’s Israeli subsidiary will step down in the wake of an inquiry that has scrutinised its business dealings with the Israeli military. stories.title: India's Modi cuts size of convoy in austerity move — reports
stories.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi has reportedly reduced the number of vehicles in his official convoy. The reports come as Modi is making an austerity appeal to Indians.

stories.title: Iran war: Oil stocks being used up at record pace, IEA warns
stories.summary: The International Energy Agency warned of "rapidly shrinking buffers." Meanwhile, Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi is en route to Delhi for a BRICS gathering which is set to be dominated by the conflict. DW has more.

stories.title: Indians growing anxious as Modi asks them to cut on fuel
stories.summary: With energy prices rising, Prime Minister Narendra Modi has urged Indians to embrace voluntary austerity to protect the economy from the fallout of the war in Iran. But residents say they are already at their limit.

stories.title: Can South Africa's President Ramaphosa avoid impeachment again?
stories.summary: Ramaphosa sidestepped an impeachment inquiry in 2022 when his ANC-dominated parlimanent blocked it. Now that South Africa's top court revived it — can he escape through a parliament he shares with former adversaries?

stories.title: Iran preparing for renewed war as military assets remain largely intact, reports warn
stories.summary: Iran is likely preparing for renewed conflict with the US and Israel, Washington-based ISW think tank assessed, citing military drills and asset repositioning after reports emerged that Tehran remained in possession of significant missile stockpiles.

stories.summary: The festival is a celebration of cinema and a frantic trade show all at once. After 25 years, I can’t help but go back Nothing prepares you for the shock that is the Cannes film festival: the adrenaline, the fatigue, the elation and the emotion, but also the hunger, the anger, the magic and the ridicule. For young cinephiles, and for almost everybody who works in the film industry, it is the mecca of cinema and has been so for nearly eight decades. Anyone going for the first time this week, as I

stories.summary: The rising star of Spanish cinema discusses being orphaned at six, new feature Romería and why she always works with children Family reunions in European arthouse cinema are almost always unhappy events, on a scale of strife that ranges from simmering resentment (Louis Malle’s Milou in May) to spectacular score-settling (Thomas Vinterberg’s Festen). stories.title: Official marking of land for Brazil’s uncontacted Kawahiva people begins after 27-year wait
stories.summary: Demarcation of 410,000 hectares of territory is intended to protect the Amazonian community from farming, illegal mining and logging More than 25 years after the existence of one of the Amazon’s most vulnerable nomadic hunter-gatherer communities was confirmed, the Brazilian government has begun demarcating the Pardo River Kawahiva Indigenous territory , giving greater protection to the uncontacted people. stories.title: US-based internet suicide forum implicated in 160 UK deaths fined £950,000
stories.summary: Ofcom attempts to block UK access to site cited in multiple coroners’ reports as it levies fine under Online Safety Act A nihilistic internet suicide forum implicated in over 160 UK deaths has been fined £950,000 by the online regulator in its latest attempt to shut it down. Biden’s visit went viral in China, with media praising his “noodle diplomacy” (one of the dishes that Biden ordered was zha

stories.title: What to know about Kyle Diamantas, the new acting FDA commissioner
stories.summary: The ex-lawyer who Trump described as ‘very talented’ was previously deputy commissioner for food at the FDA The new acting commissioner of the US Food and Drug Administration (FDA), who Donald Trump described as a “very talented person”, is a former corporate lawyer who previously defended a popular formula maker against claims of its product harming premature babies. Kyle Diamantas, who most recently served as the FDA deputy commissioner for food, will be taking over as acting FDA commissioner.

stories.title: Meta profited from illegal scam ads, California county lawsuit alleges
stories.summary: Santa Clara county claims Meta Platforms violated the state’s false advertising and unfair business practices laws California’s Santa Clara county has sued Meta Platforms, alleging it has profited from Facebook and Instagram ads promoting scams in violation of California’s false advertising and unfair business practices laws. The lawsuit – filed on Monday in Santa Clara county superior court on behalf of all California residents – accuses the social media giant of tolerating fraudulent advertisi

stories.title: France confines more than 1,700 people to cruise ship after suspected norovirus outbreak
stories.summary: About 50 people on vessel carrying mostly British and Irish passengers have symptoms, say officials French authorities have confined more than 1,700 passengers and crew members to a cruise ship docked in Bordeaux after an outbreak of suspected norovirus, officials have said. The vessel, Ambition, which is carrying 1,233 passengers, most of them British or Irish nationals, arrived in Bordeaux on Tuesday. About 50 people had shown symptoms of the virus, French health officials said. Continue readi

stories.title: More than 1,700 confined on cruise ship in Bordeaux after suspected norovirus death – Europe live
stories.summary: Operator says majority of passengers on board of cruise ship stuck are British, while about 50 people are showing symptoms Responding to the Guardian’s questions, the operator also confirmed that the vast majority of the 1,187 guests on board are British. There are also 514 crew members. Ambassador Cruise Line also confirmed that a 92-year-old man died on board earlier this week, but he did not report any symptoms at the time and the cause of his death is yet to be established. Continue reading.

stories.title: Cost of US war on Iran mounting – as it happened
stories.summary: Pete Hegseth and other officials appear before House to face grilling on Iran war expenditure and military operations Iran has expanded its definition of the strait of Hormuz into a “vast operational area” far wider than before the war, according to a senior officer in the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) Navy in comments likely to anger the US. The strait is no longer viewed as a narrow stretch around a handful of islands but instead has been greatly enlarged in scope and military signi

stories.title: Nauru moves to change its name in break from colonial past
stories.summary: The parliament of Nauru has agreed to change the South Pacific microstate's name to "Naoero". A referendum will now take place on the constitutional change.

stories.title: Is caring about the climate unmanly?
stories.summary: The answer is complex — and says a lot about how masculinity works in the modern world.

stories.title: Satellites add a new layer to global poverty data
stories.summary: A new study using satellite data finds that 58% of the global population has been assigned to the wrong tier in the UN's Human Development Index. That can have real-world consequences for how aid reaches people.

stories.title: China’s new rules give the West a new headache
stories.summary: Beijing now has strong new powers to punish firms for pulling manufacturing out of China. Multinationals are increasingly caught in a web of coercion and complex rules as they try to operate across the US, EU and China.

stories.title: Ceasefire in sight? What's next for Russia's war in Ukraine
stories.summary: Four years since Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, is the end finally in sight? DW spoke to US political experts about why they think US home elections could bring about a ceasefire.

stories.title: Cem Özdemir, first German state premier with Turkish roots
stories.summary: Cem Özdemir is the first leader of a German federal state to have Turkish roots. His Green Party won elections in the wealthy southwestern region of Baden-Württemberg.

stories.title: Israeli-Palestinian conflict: Allegations of sexual abuse
stories.summary: The issue of systemic sexual abuses as part of the Israeli-Palestinian conflict is back in the spotlight following new testimonies. Both Israel and Hamas deny the claims.

stories.title: Trump arrives in China ahead of summit with Xi Jinping
stories.summary: During high-stakes talks with China's leader, US President Donald Trump is expected to address the Iran war, trade and Taiwan. Trump posted while en route that he wants China to "open up" to US business. Follow DW.

stories.title: EU invites Taliban members to discuss Afghan migrant returns
stories.summary: The European Commission has invited officials from Afghanistan's Taliban to discuss the returns of certain migrants. It's controversial on humanitarian grounds, and because Brussels doesn't recognize Taliban authority.

stories.title: Canvas owner secures student data in deal with hacking group
stories.summary: The ShinyHunters hacking group stole student data from the popular education platform Canvas last week. Parent company Instructure apologized for the hack and said Canvas "remains safe to use."

stories.title: Pentagon says US has spent $29 billion on Iran war so far, amid concerns over weapons stockpiles
stories.summary: Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth came under intense questioning by Democratic and Republican lawmakers over the cost of war, Trump's end game and diminishing weapons stockpiles.

stories.title: China’s Unitree unveils transformable humanoid ‘mecha’ robot with cockpit
stories.summary: Chinese robotics company Unitree Robotics unveiled a transformable humanoid “mecha” robot featuring a cockpit for a human pilot.

stories.title: Trump arrives in Beijing for high-stakes summit with Xi
stories.summary: This week's trip, the first since Trump visited Beijing in 2017, will involve highly anticipated talks with Xi on Thursday and Friday.

stories.title: Barcelona star Lamine Yamal waves Palestinian flag during La Liga celebrations
stories.summary: Footage released on May 12 showed Yamal waving a Palestinian flag during FC Barcelona’s La Liga title parade celebrating the club’s 29th league title. In a news conference, Barcelona coach Hansi Flick said he did not like such gestures, but added that Yamal was old enough to make his own decisions.

stories.title: Russian cargo ship sunk off Spain carried nuclear reactors for North Korea, probe reveals
stories.summary: Spanish investigators have discovered that the Russian cargo ship that sank in the Mediterranean in 2024 — originally believed to be part of a weapons retrieval mission to Syria — was secretly carrying nuclear reactor components bound for North Korea.

stories.title: Cannes Film Festival 2026: Fans lock down red carpet spots ahead of opening
stories.summary: Film fans gather overnight at Cannes, using stepladders and chains to secure red carpet spots as they wait for a glimpse of opening night stars.

stories.title: Australian property developer says Trump hotel plan scrapped due to 'toxic' brand
stories.summary: The Australian developer's statement followed a report in the Australian Financial Review that the Trump Organisation had pulled out of the deal.

stories.title: Iran's economy is being tested by war, blockades and soaring inflation
stories.summary: Inflation has hit 54% and the rial has lost more than half its value, but Tehran insists it can hold out

stories.title: Shrinking Milka chocolate bar tricked consumers, says German court
stories.summary: A court in Bremen has found the manufacturer of the classic Alpine Milk chocolate bar guilty of "shrinkflation".

stories.title: Gaza sisters win prize for turning rubble into reusable bricks
stories.summary: Displaced teenagers Farah and Tala wanted to 'turn destruction into something useful'.

stories.title: Climbers clear path to Everest summit past giant chunk of ice
stories.summary: Nearly 1,000 climbers will attempt to scale the peak in coming weeks and this has raised safety concerns.

stories.title: More than 1,000 passengers held on cruise after gastrointestinal illness outbreak
stories.summary: The ship, which set sail from Belfast on Friday, is at port in Bordeaux after 49 people fell ill from gastrointestinal illness.

stories.title: What to know as Trump visits Xi in China
stories.summary: Trump has landed in Beijing for the first visit by a US leader since his own in 2017.

stories.title: Watch: UK Parliament revives centuries-old ritual before King’s Speech
stories.summary: Black Rod was symbolically denied entry to the House of Commons during the ceremonial opening of the UK Parliament ahead of King Charles III’s speech.

stories.title: Thousands of Waymos recalled after robotaxi swept into a creek
stories.summary: The voluntary recall follows an incident on 20 April where an empty Waymo car entered a flooded road in San Antonio, Texas.

stories.title: Epstein abused me while under house arrest, survivor tells US lawmakers
stories.summary: Roza was among several victims who gave testimony to Democratic lawmakers on Tuesday.

stories.title: Trump arrives in China for high-stakes meeting with Xi Jinping
stories.summary: The leaders of the global superpowers are expected to discuss the war in Iran, tariffs, AI and Taiwan during the two-day visit.

stories.title: Woman isolating on British island in South Pacific after hantavirus contact
stories.summary: The woman who was on a virus-hit ship shows "no signs of illness", the government of Pitcairn Islands, a British Overseas Territory, says.

stories.title: Tens of thousands march in Brussels during Belgium national strike
stories.summary: Tens of thousands of protesters marched through Brussels during a national strike called by Belgian unions against the government’s fiscal reforms.

stories.title: Chaos in Philippines as Duterte ally wanted by ICC takes refuge in senate to avoid arrest
stories.summary: Former police chief Ronald dela Rosa spends night at senate office after another Duterte ally offers protective custody The unusual pursuit was captured on CCTV cameras inside the Philippine senate. Ronald dela Rosa, a longtime ally of the former president Rodrigo Duterte, raced along the hallways of the upper house complex, stumbling on the staircase, as he fled government agents. “They want to forcibly bring me to The Hague, to surrender me there,” Dela Rosa said later on a Facebook livestream

stories.title: The Kabul rehab centre hit by deadly Pakistani strike
stories.summary: Pakistan says it hit 'military and terrorist infrastructure' - but the UN and victims' families reject this claim.

stories.title: UAE’s secret attack on Iran risks drawing Gulf states into the war
stories.summary: If current precarious ceasefire between US and Iran ends, Emirates are more likely to be targeted by Tehran The risk of some Gulf states becoming embroiled in a direct war with Iran has risen after it was reported the United Arab Emirates had secretly launched a major attack on Iran during the conflict. In addition, Kuwait has said that at least four members of the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps had been captured trying to carry out “terrorist attacks” on the Kuwaiti-owned Bubiyan Island, the

stories.title: Hotter 2026 and El Nino could trigger extreme fires
stories.summary: More than 150 million hectares — over twice the size of Texas — burned globally in the first months of 2026. With a high chance of a supercharged El Nino, the second half of the year could be even worse.

stories.title: Iran: Surge of political prisoners amid US-Israel war
stories.summary: Amid the US-Israel war with Iran, as the world breathlessly watches the Strait of Hormuz, Tehran is quietly increasing pressure on political prisoners. Advocates are sounding the alarm, warning of mass executions.

stories.title: OpenAI boss Sam Altman says Musk wanted control of company
stories.summary: Sam Altman testified in trial that could determine the future of OpenAI, the company behind ChatGPT.

stories.title: Peacocks take over northern Italian beach town
stories.summary: More than 100 peacocks now roam Punta Marina in northern Italy, leaving residents searching for humane ways to manage the birds.

stories.title: Zelensky's ex-chief of staff in court as Ukraine corruption probe escalates
stories.summary: Andriy Yermak was named by Ukraine's two anti-corruption agencies as a suspect in a money-laundering scheme.

stories.title: No sign of larger hantavirus outbreak, says UN health agency
stories.summary: The situation could still change and there might be more confirmed cases, warns the head of the World Health Organization.

stories.title: Kazakhstan launches driverless Light Rail Transit system after 10 years of delays
stories.summary: After a decade of construction delays, Astana has launched its Light Rail Transit (LRT) system, featuring driverless overground trains serving 18 stations across the city.

stories.title: SOCAR acquisition of Italy’s Italiana Petroli finalised after EU clearance
stories.summary: SOCAR has completed its acquisition of Italiana Petroli after securing all regulatory approvals, marking a major expansion of its downstream presence in Italy and strengthening its position in Europe’s energy market.

stories.title: Palestinians gather to mark 78th anniversary of the Nakba and call for 'right of return'
stories.summary: Palestinians in the occupied West Bank and the Gaza Strip marked the 78th anniversary of the Nakba, a defining moment in Palestinian history dating back to 1948, when hundreds of thousands of people were forced to flee their homes, villages and land, becoming refugees.

stories.title: Lebanon says two paramedics among 13 killed in Israeli strikes
stories.summary: Lebanon's health ministry says the paramedics were carrying out a rescue mission in Nabatieh in response to an earlier attack that killed one person.

stories.title: Australia has some of the world's costliest homes. Will scrapping tax breaks help?
stories.summary: The government hopes reforms will help young people enter the market, while critics say it will stifle supply.

stories.title: A decade on, Trump returns to a stronger and more assertive China
stories.summary: Beijing is arguably the most powerful competitor the US has confronted in its history, one analyst says.

stories.title: Air India crisis deepens ahead of final Ahmedabad crash report
stories.summary: Air India faces a leadership vacuum and mounting financial losses as it struggles to recover from the crash.

stories.title: Jason Collins, NBA's first openly gay player, dies aged 47
stories.summary: Collins shared late last year that he had been diagnosed with an aggressive form of brain cancer.

stories.title: Trump's 'Golden Dome' will cost $1.2tn and might not stop all-out missile attack
stories.summary: That pricetag is nearly seven times higher than Trump's initial estimate, found an independent budget office.

stories.title: Majdnem egy évvel a cicaharcuk után Elon Musk Kínába megy Donald Trumppal
stories.summary: Közel húsz üzletember csatlakozik az amerikai elnök kínai delegációjához.

stories.title: A svéd katonáknak esélyük sem volt az ukrán drónpilóták elleni hadgyakorlaton
stories.summary: Ukrán katonák szerint háromszor is megállították a gyakorlatot, hogy a svédek át tudják gondolni a következő lépésüket.

stories.title: Harka egyik utcájába a futárok, sőt a szemétszállítók sem hajthatnak be átmenetileg azbesztszennyezés miatt
stories.summary: Bevizsgáltatták Harkán a murvás utakat, azonnali döntéseket is hozott a képviselő-testület.

stories.title: Kiderült, hogy mennyi hasznot hoztak az adófizetőknek az állami tőkebefektetések: szinte semmit
stories.summary: A Nemzeti Tőkeholding friss adatai szerint az állami cégek az elmúlt 14 évben 1285 milliárd forintot helyeztek el magántőkealapokban, volt olyan befektetés, amit húsz évre kötöttek le.

stories.title: Megvan a vagyonalap, jöhet a következő szint. Hova érdemes innen lépni?
stories.summary: Ez a történet nem a nulláról induló pénzügyi tudatosságról szól, hanem arról a pontról, ahol már van mit védeni, optimalizálni és továbbépíteni. Stabil egzisztencia, több lábon álló – de változó – jövedelem, és egy élethelyzet, ahol a döntések tétje már nem kicsi. Itt lép be a képbe a CIB Bank szakértői háttere: személyre szabott gondolkodással, rendszerezett tanácsokkal és olyan eszközökkel, amik segítenek átlátni, merre érdemes mozdulni.

stories.title: Görög Zita vécékefébe énekelve gyűjt adományt hátrányos helyzetű gyerekeknek
stories.summary: Tavaly Birkin táskákkal kampányolt, idén más az aktualitás.

stories.title: Afrika hamarosan kettészakadhat
stories.summary: Igaz, a hamarosant itt földtörténeti léptékben kell érteni, szóval még ha meg is történik, van pár millió évünk.

stories.title: A Feszty-körképnél lesz az első kormányülés
stories.summary: Egy-másfél órára tervezik a Tisza-kormány első kormányülését – tudta meg lapunk.

stories.title: Gajdos László azonnal leállítja a fakivágásokat a védett erdőkben
stories.summary: Az élő környezetért felelős miniszter nem engedi tovább a tarvágásokat.

stories.title: Megmutatta a Fidesz, mire gondoltak, amikor konstruktív ellenzékiséget ígértek a parlamentben
stories.summary: A bizottsági meghallgatások végén a Fideszt keményebben kiosztó miniszterjelöltekre nemmel szavaztak, de a jelöltek többségénél tartózkodtak az ellenzéki szerepbe szorult fideszesek. Sőt, voltak olyan jelöltek, akiknél még megengedőbbek voltak.

stories.title: Nyomkövetőt kap, és házi őrizetbe kerül Bóna Zoltán volt fideszes országgyűlési képviselő
stories.summary: A gyanú szerint arról állapodott meg Halásztelek polgármesterével, hogy annak édesanyját személyi titkáraként alkalmazza. A 70 éves nő semmilyen munkát nem végzett, de a pénzt visszaosztotta a politikusnak.

stories.title: A győri polgármester szerint rendőrnyomozók szálltak ki a Győr-Szolhoz
stories.summary: Pintér Bence szerint adatgyűjtést végeznek.

stories.title: Az aszály miatt nem kaszál annyit a Főkert
stories.summary: A Főkert májusban csak ott kaszál rendszeresen, ahol közlekedésbiztonsági okokból indokolt.

stories.title: New York Times: Irán alaposan összekapta magát, egyre több rakétaindítója működik
stories.summary: Hírszerzési források alapján arról írt a lap, még a Hormuzi-szorosban sem sikerült szétzúzni az ország rakétaképességeit.

stories.title: A választás óta már nincs kedvük annyit posztolni a megafonos influenszereknek
stories.summary: A Lakmusz szerint a vizsgált megafonosok posztszáma a választás előtti szint kevesebb mint ötödére csökkent.

stories.title: Hankó államtitkára is távozott az NKA bizottságából, a tagság fele már lemondott
stories.summary: Kiss-Hegyi Anita a korábbi Kulturális és Innovációs Minisztérium államtitkára volt, Hankó Balázs idén januárban nevezte ki az NKA bizottsági tagjának.

stories.title: Lemondatná elnöki tisztségéről a fideszes Szűcs Lajost az országos horgászszövetség választmánya
stories.summary: A május végi közgyűlés napirendi pontjai közé került Szűcs felmentése.

stories.title: Egy út, egy jegy, teljes jogok – indul a nemzetközi vonatutak uniós reformja
stories.summary: Az Európai Bizottság olyan javaslatokat mutatott be, amelyekkel több társaság vonatai között átszállva is elég lenne egyszer jegyet venni az egész útra, és kiterjesztenék az utasjogokat.

stories.title: Nagyszabású dróntámadást indítottak az oroszok Ukrajna ellen, többen megsérültek
stories.summary: Az ukrán beszámolók szerint több hullámban, legalább 200 kamikazedrónt indítottak az ukrajnai kritikus és civil infrastruktúra ellen.

stories.title: Trump a korábbinál gyengébb pozícióból tárgyal, de sakkban tudják tartani egymást a kínai elnökkel
stories.summary: Az iráni háború árnyéka vetül Trump kétnapos pekingi csúcstalálkozójára Hszi Csin-pinggel. Főleg a két ország közti kereskedelmi kapcsolatok lesznek középpontban, de az utolsó pillanatban bejött egy olyan téma, ami meghatározhatja a következő éveket.

stories.title: Gulyás Gergelynek a kommunista diktatúra jutott eszébe Sulyok Tamás mellőzéséről
stories.summary: Antall Józsefet felidézve elmélkedett ismét azon, hogy szerinte nem szép dolog állami hivatalok vezetőit és a köztársasági elnököt lemondásra kényszeríteni.

stories.title: Feloszlathatják az izraeli parlamentet
stories.summary: Az izraeli kormánykoalíció egyik ultraortodox pártja az ultraortodox férfiak kötelező katonai szolgálata miatt fenyeget a lépéssel.

stories.title: Örülnek a cirkuszok, hogy Gajdos László minisztériuma kitiltja a vadállatokat a cirkuszokból
stories.summary: Lehet, hogy az utolsó fellépő indiai elefántnak, Szandrának is megvan az új lakhelye.

stories.title: Ópusztaszerre menet posztoltak fotókat a Tisza-kormány miniszterei, az aszályhelyzetet is megnézték
stories.summary: Kapitány István, Tanács Zoltán és az új kormányszóvivők is posztoltak az útról és a kormányülés előtti, Maris pusztai megállóról is.

stories.title: Kárpátalját minden korábbinál több orosz drón érte el, amint véget ért a Moszkva által kért tűzszünet
stories.summary: Oroszország Ukrajna-szerte dróntámadást indított, az ország energetikai hálózatát célozva.

stories.title: A Fideszt is kritizáló fideszes Cser-Palkovics András készen áll a közös munkára Vitézy Dáviddal
stories.summary: A székesfehérvári polgármester levélben gratulált Vitézynek, és rögtön fel is vetett két fontos témát.

stories.title: Megérkezett a kormány Ópusztaszerre
stories.summary: A kormány első ülésén feltűnt Tóth Péter, a Tisza korábbi kampányfőnöke is, aki nemrég a visszavonulását jelentette be.

stories.title: Rendszerváltást a közlekedésben is!
stories.summary: A politika után a közlekedésben is rendszerváltásra van szükség, mert az elmúlt évtizedek közlekedéspolitikája nem a közjót, hanem az autó uralmát szolgálta.

stories.title: EU preparing to offer key concession to UK in new post-Brexit agricultural deal
stories.summary: Exclusive: Britain expected to be allowed to keep ban on live animal exports, sources say, in fillip for Keir Starmer UK politics live – latest updates Brussels is preparing to offer Keir Starmer a key concession in talks over an agricultural deal, giving the beleaguered prime minister an important victory in his efforts to move closer to the EU. European officials have conceded that the UK can keep its ban on live animal exports as part of any joint deal on food and agricultural products, accor

stories.title: Russia launches strikes at Ukraine, hours after expiration of 72-hour truce
stories.summary: On Sunday, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said Ukraine would retaliate if Russia decided "to return to a full-scale war."

stories.title: A weakened Trump to test China's appetite for deal-making
stories.summary: A quagmire in Iran, sky-high oil prices and shrinking support at home: when US President Donald Trump belatedly travels to China this week, his position has weakened. Still, he is hoping for any deal. Nervous Europeans are watching.

stories.title: Ukraine: EU sanctions Russians over 'systematic unlawful deportation' of children
stories.summary: The EU is sanctioning 16 individuals and seven entities over alleged child abductions. The UK also issued fresh sanctions, and Germany's defense minister visited Kyiv amid a shaky ceasefire. DW has the latest.

stories.title: Drug gang attacks ‘force hundreds of Indigenous families to flee’ in Mexico
stories.summary: Violence in Guerrero state has driven as many as 1,000 households from their homes, rights group says Hundreds of Indigenous families have been forced to flee their homes in the mountains of central Mexico by intense attacks from a local criminal group, including drone bombings, an Indigenous rights organisation said on Monday . A gang known as Los Ardillos has been carrying out attacks in Guerrero state for years, but they started to intensify last week. Villages were subjected to eight hours o

stories.title: Yes, Europe’s leaders are finally standing up to Trump. Here’s why | Mujtaba Rahman
stories.summary: Friedrich Merz’s criticism of the US president was not a solo run. It was born of the realisation that US leverage has slipped Friedrich Merz’s criticism of Donald Trump last month reflected more than a moment of personal candour or a split between Berlin and the White House. It pointed to a broader shift under way among European leaders. Increasingly they are willing to publicly confront the Trump administration on issues ranging from Iran to Ukraine and European sovereignty. The Trump administ

stories.title: Gaborone gold rush: how Botswana rose to the top of men’s sprinting
stories.summary: Country with a population of just 2.5m credits investment in young athletes for its rise but this progress is under threat It was a fairytale ending to the World Athletics Relays in Gaborone. In the final strait, Collen Kebinatshipi surged past South Africa’s Zakithi Nene to win the men’s 4x400m relay for Botswana. The home crowd, a sea of light blue, went wild. “It means so many things to us,” Letsile Tebogo, 22, the reigning 200m Olympic champion, who ran the second leg, told reporters afterwa

stories.title: Internal displacements caused by violence or conflict at record high in 2025
stories.summary: The 32.3m surpasses those caused by disasters for the first time, as 82.2m people displaced in total around world The number of internal displacements triggered by conflict or violence around the world reached a record high in 2025, surpassing the number of disaster-driven internal displacements for the first time. A report published by the Internal Displacement Monitoring Centre (IDMC) shows that by the end of 2025 there were 32.3m conflict-driven internal displacements. That is 60% higher than

stories.title: More than 110 Nobel laureates call on Iran to release gravely ill activist Narges Mohammadi
stories.summary: As human rights advocate is treated in Tehran hospital after transfer from Zanjan prison, prize winners demand her freedom More than 110 Nobel laureates have called for the immediate and unconditional release of Narges Mohammadi, the imprisoned Iranian human rights activist and Nobel peace prize laureate, after she was transferred to hospital amid concerns over her rapidly deteriorating health. In a statement released on Tuesday, 112 Nobel laureates urged the Iranian authorities and the internat

stories.title: Hantavirus: How is the outbreak being contained?
stories.summary: Passengers evacuated from the MV Hondius cruise ship are returning to their home countries, some with symptoms, many without. What happens to these people and what is being done to contain the spread?

stories.title: Ukrainian children held in Russia: militarized, 'reeducated'
stories.summary: A Ukrainian initiative to repatriate abducted Ukrainian children reports that at least 20,000 girls and boys are being held by Russian families and authorities. Can these children ever find their way home?

stories.title: EU negotiators agree new rules on vital medicines
stories.summary: A new agreement aims to tackle the drug shortages that have plagued the bloc. Among other things, it would make it easier to use public funds to support the production of essential medicines.

stories.title: What now for the role of high representative in Bosnia?
stories.summary: Christian Schmidt's departure as high representative in Bosnia and Herzegovina doesn't look like a routine diplomatic handover. It seems to indicate that international powers want something different from the role.

stories.title: Hijab-wearing German beauty contestant takes on the AfD
stories.summary: As a Miss Germany contestant, Büsra Sayed wore a hijab on stage. This had never happened before — and the far-right AfD didn't like it. Her response went viral.

stories.title: UK: Keir Starmer tells Cabinet he will continue in place, as four junior ministers quit
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has met with his Cabinet amid continued pressure after election losses. A set piece speech yesterday failed to calm the waters, and four junior ministers resigned on Tuesday.

stories.title: Man charged with attempting to kill US President Donald Trump pleads not guilty
stories.summary: Besides the attempted-assassination count, Cole Tomas Allen is also charged with assaulting a federal officer with a deadly weapon and two additional firearms counts.

stories.title: 'Total lack of respect': Macron interrupts speaker to ask for silence
stories.summary: The French president stood up during a conference in Kenya to tell the audience to quieten down, saying it was “impossible” for speakers to be heard.

stories.title: Pashinyan says Karabakh 'was not ours' as Armenia and Azerbaijan look to future
stories.summary: In an extraordinary admission, Armenia’s PM Nikol Pashinyan declared that Karabakh was not Armenia’s territory and that the Armenian movement for it was “a fatal mistake,” as Yerevan forges its peace with Azerbaijan and its decisive pro-EU course.

stories.title: International Feline Contest: Europe’s 'most beautiful cats' gather in Sofia
stories.summary: Cat lovers from across Europe gathered in Sofia, Bulgaria, for the international Cat Show Expo, where more than 150 cats competed in a prestigious feline competition judged by experts from several countries.

stories.title: I led hikers up an Indonesian volcano - and then it erupted
stories.summary: An Indonesian guide who led hikers up Mount Dukono has described the moment of Friday's eruption which killed three people.

stories.title: Why welfare isn't winning elections in India like it used to
stories.summary: India's welfare politics still matters, but voters increasingly want jobs, growth and dignity too.

stories.title: Iranian chief negotiator says US must accept peace proposal or face 'failure'
stories.summary: Iran has refused to back down in its war with Washington, with military officials warning they are prepared to respond to any renewed US attack.

stories.title: German ultrarunner Arda Saatçi completes 600-kilometre Death Valley to Los Angeles challenge
stories.summary: German athlete Arda Saatçi completed a 600-kilometre ultramarathon challenge from Death Valley to Los Angeles after running for more than 123 hours.

stories.title: Final passengers disembark hantavirus-hit cruise ship in Tenerife
stories.summary: The final passengers aboard the hantavirus-hit cruise ship MV Hondius disembarked in Tenerife before boarding flights to quarantine facilities in more than 20 countries.

stories.title: Macron thanks Kipchoge for 'patience' after Nairobi morning run
stories.summary: Macron joined marathon legend Eliud Kipchoge for an early morning run in Nairobi on day three of his Africa tour, later thanking the two-time Olympic champion for his "patience."

stories.title: At least nine killed after bomb rigged to rickshaw explodes at north Pakistan bazaar
stories.summary: The Pakistani Taliban and other militant groups have grown more emboldened since the Afghan Taliban returned to power in Kabul in 2021.

stories.title: Iran threatens weapons-grade uranium enrichment as peace talks falter
stories.summary: Tehran threatened to enrich uranium to weapons-grade if attacked again, as Trump said the ceasefire had a "one percent chance" of surviving after rejecting Tehran's proposal.

stories.title: Qatar warns Iran against weaponising Hormuz to 'blackmail' Gulf countries
stories.summary: Qatar warned Tehran on Tuesday against using the Strait of Hormuz as a "weapon" to hold Gulf states to ransom, as Doha and Ankara backed ongoing mediation efforts to end the Iran war and restore access to the vital waterway.

stories.title: Chinese people divided ahead of Trump-Xi summit in Beijing
stories.summary: Residents in Beijing shared mixed opinions ahead of the upcoming meeting between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping, with some hoping for improved ties and others expressing skepticism.

stories.title: Angela Rayner calls blocking Andy Burnham’s return to parliament a mistake as pressure mounts on Keir Starmer – as it happened
stories.summary: Catherine West issues ultimatum for PM as ex-minister Josh Simons joins calls for prime minister to quit At the start of her programme Laura Kuenssberg addressed Catherine West and Bridget Phillipson who were sitting waiting for the main interviews. Kuenssberg told West she wanted a cabinet minister to challenge Keir Starmer. She said she was sitting next to one of them. What was her message to her? Well, there’s nothing stopping Bridget from standing. Why are all the men better than the women?

stories.title: Europe should behave more like China does if it wants to survive this age of chaos
stories.summary: Instead of obsessing over rules that have ceased to matter, we must consider giving Beijing a dose of its own medicine The US and Israel may have started the war in Iran, but – apart from the belligerents themselves – it is China and Europe that stand to lose the most from it. Yet while European leaders watch like rabbits caught in the headlights as energy prices shoot through the roof, China has responded to the crisis with remarkable equanimity. It is striking how self-confident Beijing is ahe

stories.title: Germany news: 1-in-6 retailers fear for future
stories.summary: About one-in-12 German businesses worry about survival, with twice as many retailers jittery. Meanwhile in Stuttgart, five pro-Palestinian activists face trial over attacking an Israeli defense company site. Follow DW.

stories.title: French farmers dump manure at TotalEnergies refinery in fuel price protest
stories.summary: French farmers have staged a protest outside a major oil refinery near Lyon, dumping manure and rubbish at the entrance in anger over rising fuel costs and falling incomes.

stories.title: Sudan drone strikes killed at least 880 civilians between January and April, UN says
stories.summary: Most of the civilian deaths attributed to drone strikes in the first three months of the year were recorded in the Kordofan region and Darfur, although the strikes were also increasingly spreading beyond both regions.

stories.title: Body of missing US soldier recovered off coast of Morocco
stories.summary: 1st Lt Kendrick Lamont Key Jr was participating in military exercise among US, Nato allies and African countries A search team recovered the body of a US soldier who went missing near a cliff during a training exercise in Cap Draa, Morocco, the US army said on Sunday. Moroccan searchers found the remains on Saturday in the water within a mile (1.6km) of where the soldier went missing on 2 May, the army said in a statement. Continue reading...

stories.title: Hantavirus cruise ship passengers 'remain asymptomatic' before flights home – video
stories.summary: Spanish health minister Monica Garcia says all those onboard a hantavirus-stricken cruise ship were 'asymptomatic' after it arrived in Tenerife in the Canary Islands on Sunday. Everyone disembarking will be checked for symptoms, authorities say, and be taken off the ship only once evacuation flights are ready to fly them to their destinations Argentina in spotlight over hantavirus as authorities retrace footsteps of ship’s passengers Dozens of people from cruise ship struck by hantavirus leave T

stories.title: Britain and Ireland’s wildflowers – in pictures
stories.summary: The Eden Project’s National Wildflower Centre is opening entries for its Wildflower Photographer of the Year 2026 competition on 29 May. The contest showcases images of some of Britain and Ireland’s 1,600 wildflower species, and a selection of photos from last year’s competition will be on display at Eden Dock, Canary Wharf, London, during CWG’s Nature Week, from 13 July Continue reading...

stories.title: Weather tracker: US and Mexico brace for heatwave as deadly floods hit South Africa
stories.summary: Temperatures soar in California and Arizona, while deluge continues across Western and Northern Cape Heat is expected to intensify across western parts of the US and Mexico this week as a ridge of high pressure pushes temperatures well above the seasonal norm. Daytime highs are forecast to reach 10-15C above average in some areas. The US National Weather Service has issued heat advisories for parts of California and Arizona, with extreme heat warnings in force on Monday and Tuesday in places suc

stories.title: Germany: 'Ulm 5' on trial after attack on Israeli arms firm
stories.summary: Five activists have gone on trial in Germany following a break-in at the Israeli arms company Elbit Systems in Ulm. Their lawyers intend to argue they were trying to obstruct a genocide.

stories.title: Could German ex-leader really negotiate Ukraine peace?
stories.summary: Former German chancellor Gerhard Schröder is a controversial — and some critics would even say, unserious — choice as a negotiator on the Ukraine war. He's long been criticized for being too close to Russia.

stories.title: Why is Hollywood missing from Cannes?
stories.summary: There are no US blockbusters premiering at the Cannes Film Festival this year. So who's snubbing who?

stories.title: Germany's businesses frustrated by economic decline
stories.summary: No one had higher hopes for Friedrich Merz's chancellorship than the German business community. Now, however, early euphoria has given way to bitter disappointment.

stories.title: Somali piracy disrupts global shipping and trade routes
stories.summary: As ships reroute around Africa to avoid Middle East conflict zones, piracy off Somalia is back. The resurgence is driving up insurance, transit times and security costs, putting new pressure on global supply chains.

stories.title: Endemic, epidemic, pandemic: What's the difference?
stories.summary: The Andes hantavirus outbreak shows how easily disease labels are misunderstood — and why they describe patterns of spread, not how dangerous a virus is.

stories.title: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer 'not walking away' after local election mauling
stories.summary: Keir Starmer has said he takes "responsibility" for Labour's poor election performance, but also for putting matters right again. "I know I have my doubters and I know I need to prove them wrong, and I will," he said.

stories.title: Hantavirus: How it differs from COVID
stories.summary: In 2018-2019, the Andes virus hantavirus spread person-to-person in Argentina. Eleven people died — but it was stopped. Analysis of that outbreak shows the current one can also be stopped.

stories.title: Architecture of remembrance and hope: Daniel Libeskind at 80
stories.summary: Famous for designing the Jewish Museum Berlin and the Ground Zero project in New York, the architect has created dozens of memorable buildings worldwide.

stories.title: How hotels are stopping the 'dawn dash' for sunbeds after man wins payout
stories.summary: Some resorts enforce allocation rules from check-in to prevent people reserving loungers with towels in the holiday sunbed scramble.

stories.title: Tourist hotspot at 'end of the world' denies causing hantavirus outbreak
stories.summary: The BBC reports from the city of Ushuaia, where experts have been sent to investigate the origins of the outbreak.
